question: Why does batch normalization speed up training of deep networks?
reference_answer: Batch normalization normalizes layer inputs per mini-batch, which reduces internal covariate shift and smooths the loss landscape, allowing higher learning rates and faster, more stable convergence.

criteria.text: States that batch normalization normalizes layer activations using mini-batch statistics (mean and variance)
Explains that normalization reduces internal covariate shift across layers during training
Explains that it smooths the optimization landscape, permitting higher learning rates

question: What are the main limitations of using reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF)?
reference_answer: RLHF is limited by the cost and noise of human preference data, reward model overoptimization (reward hacking), and instability of the policy-optimization stage.

criteria.text: Identifies the cost and noisiness of collecting human preference data as a limitation
Identifies reward model overoptimization / reward hacking as a limitation
